data.description: Tested versions Reproducible in: Godot Engine v4.5.stable.official Not reproducible in: Godot v4.4.1 System information Godot v4.5.stable - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+)...

    <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"DiscussionForumPosting","headline":"Autoload naming broken in 4.5","articleBody":"### Tested versions\n\n- Reproducible in: Godot Engine v4.5.stable.official\n- Not reproducible in: Godot v4.4.1\n\n### System information\n\nGodot v4.5.stable - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Vega 11 Graphics (RADV RAVEN) - AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics (8 threads) - 5.71 GiB memory\n\n### Issue description\n\nAutoloads are not given a class name in PascalCase.\n\nin 4.4 and earlier versions, autoload script files would be written in `snake_case`, and automatically given a class name in `PascalCase`. now script files are no longer being renamed in autoload, leaving them in snake_case and forcing us to rename them, this causes both names to appear when typing until godot is restarted.\n\nbehaviour in 4.5 (I renamed `combat_manager` to `CombatManager`, you have to look at `player`, which should be `Player`):\n\u003cimg width=\"848\" height=\"92\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73459a7c-614b-4ce7-8449-51ae1c9567f2\" /\u003e\n\nhow it's supposed to look like (4.4.1):\n\n\u003cimg width=\"848\" height=\"92\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/370859f8-2f72-483a-bb37-f3b414963a72\" /\u003e\n\ngiving files a name in `PascalCase` (MyScript.gd) would be against the gdscript style, as files must be in `snake_case` and class names in `PascalCase`:\nhttps://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_styleguide.html#naming-conventions\n\n### Steps to reproduce\n\n1 - go to project settings-\u003eautoloads\n2 - create a new script file, give it a name in snake_case (my_autoload.gd)\n3 - create a new script on a node and try to access the autoload:\n\n`func _ready() -\u003e void:`\n4 - the suggestion will appear in snake_case\n\n[optional]\n5 - rename it in PascalCase (MyAutoload)\n6 - now both names are suggested (snake_case and PascalCase), until godot is restarted.\n\n### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)\n\nN/A","author":{"url":"https://github.com/Jesusemora","@type":"Person","name":"Jesusemora"},"datePublished":"2025-09-25T18:59:07.000Z","interactionStatistic":{"@type":"InteractionCounter","interactionType":"https://schema.org/CommentAction","userInteractionCount":3},"url":"https://github.com/110908/godot/issues/110908"}</script>

GiB memory\n\n### Issue description\n\nAutoloads are not given a class name in PascalCase.\n\nin 4.4 and earlier versions, autoload script files would be written in `snake_case`, and automatically autoload:\n\n`func _ready() -\u003e void:`\n4 - the suggestion will appear in snake_case\n\n[optional]\n5 - rename it in PascalCase (MyAutoload)\n6 - now both names are suggested (snake_case and I went ahead and looked into this and it appears this was an intentional change made in #108642 to fix #108575, which is someone requesting that the node matches what the user asked for.\n\nI'll note that the field you're entering `new_file` into is a `Node` name, not a file name, and so should be pascal cased according to the linked guidelines. Indeed, if you enter `NewFile`, you'll see that everything works as expected: the file is still snake cased automatically and the node name is pascal cased as you want it to be.","bodyHTML":"\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI can reproduce the behavior difference, but it does seem reasonable to me that if you're asking for snake case you receive snake case. data-team-hovercards-enabled="true" class="markdown-body" data-turbolinks="false"><div class="markdown-body NewMarkdownViewer-module__safe-html-box__ZT1eD"><h3 dir="auto">Tested versions</h3>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Reproducible in: Godot Engine v4.5.stable.official</li>
<li>Not reproducible in: Godot v4.4.1</li>
</ul>
<h3 dir="auto">System information</h3>
<p dir="auto">Godot v4.5.stable - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Vega 11 Graphics (RADV RAVEN) - AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics (8 threads) - 5.71 GiB memory</p>
<h3 dir="auto">Issue description</h3>
<p dir="auto">Autoloads are not given a class name in PascalCase.</p>
<p dir="auto">in 4.4 and earlier versions, autoload script files would be written in <code class="notranslate">snake_case</code>, and automatically given a class name in <code class="notranslate">PascalCase</code>. now script files are no longer being renamed in autoload, leaving them in snake_case and forcing us to rename them, this causes both names to appear when typing until godot is restarted.</p>
<p dir="auto">behaviour in 4.5 (I renamed <code class="notranslate">combat_manager</code> to <code class="notranslate">CombatManager</code>, you have to look at <code class="notranslate">player</code>, which should be <code class="notranslate">Player</code>):<br>
<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/32273722/494083693-73459a7c-614b-4ce7-8449-51ae1c9567f2.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..TUbFaCbkdNbj7pOswjf6_MsVPsrNSGoY4Q3kHtSx5Fc"><img width="848" height="92" alt="Image" src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/32273722/494083693-73459a7c-614b-4ce7-8449-51ae1c9567f2.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..TUbFaCbkdNbj7pOswjf6_MsVPsrNSGoY4Q3kHtSx5Fc" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 92px;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto">how it's supposed to look like (4.4.1):</p>
<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/32273722/494084137-370859f8-2f72-483a-bb37-f3b414963a72.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..g9bwysi5NPhKQHRb29jZtVi_hw2pn21VmACV3LQ9-QA"><img width="848" height="92" alt="Image" src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/32273722/494084137-370859f8-2f72-483a-bb37-f3b414963a72.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..g9bwysi5NPhKQHRb29jZtVi_hw2pn21VmACV3LQ9-QA" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 92px;"></a>
<p dir="auto">giving files a name in <code class="notranslate">PascalCase</code> (MyScript.gd) would be against the gdscript style, as files must be in <code class="notranslate">snake_case</code> and class names in <code class="notranslate">PascalCase</code>:<br>
<a href="https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_styleguide.html#naming-conventions" rel="nofollow">https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_styleguide.html#naming-conventions</a></p>
<h3 dir="auto">Steps to reproduce</h3>
<p dir="auto">1 - go to project settings->autoloads<br>
2 - create a new script file, give it a name in snake_case (my_autoload.gd)<br>
3 - create a new script on a node and try to access the autoload:</p>
<p dir="auto"><code class="notranslate">func _ready() -> void:</code><br>
4 - the suggestion will appear in snake_case</p>
<p dir="auto">[optional]<br>
5 - rename it in PascalCase (MyAutoload)<br>
6 - now both names are suggested (snake_case and PascalCase), until godot is restarted.</p>
<h3 dir="auto">Minimal reproduction project (MRP)</h3>
<p dir="auto">N/A</p></div></div><div class="IssueBodyViewer-module__IssueBodyTaskList__a_Inr"><div role="toolbar" aria-label="Reactions" class="d-flex gap-1 flex-wrap"><button type="button" <li>create new script, default name is <code class="notranslate">new_script.gd</code></li>

can reproduce the behavior difference, but it does seem reasonable to me that if you're asking for snake case you receive snake case. I went ahead and looked into this and it appears this was an intentional change made in <a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="3233452050" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/108642" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/godotengine/godot/pull/108642/hovercard" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108642" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#108642</a> to fix <a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="3226433217" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/108575" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/godotengine/godot/issues/108575/hovercard" href="https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/108575" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#108575</a>, which is someone requesting that the node matches what the user asked for.</p>
<p dir="auto">I'll note that the field you're entering <code class="notranslate">new_file</code> into is a <code class="notranslate">Node</code> name, not a file name, and so should be pascal cased according to the linked guidelines. Indeed, if you enter <code class="notranslate">NewFile</code>, you'll see that everything works as expected: the file is still snake cased automatically and the node name is pascal cased as you want it to be.</p></div></div><div role="toolbar" aria-label="Reactions" class="d-flex gap-1 flex-wrap"><button type="button" aria-label="👍 3 reactions" role="switch" aria-checked="false"
